sections.paragraph:
not used in Kal (compare תָּאַב No. II).

Piel תִּעֵב-

(1) to abhor, to abominate, Deu 7:26 Job 9:31 19:19 Psa 5:7 Isa 49:7.

(2) causat. to cause to be abhorred, to occasion horror to any one, Isa 49:7, מְתָעֵב גֹּוי “who causeth abhorrence to the people,” who is held in abomination by the people.

(3) to make abominable, Eze 16:25, see Hiphil.

Hiphil, to make abominable, shameful, Psa 14:1. הִתְעִיבוּ עֲלִילָה “they have basely done their deed,” they acted shamefully; hence without עֲלִילָה id. 1Ki 21:26 Eze 16:52. Compare הֵרִעַ הִשְׁחִית.

Niphal, pass. to be held in abomination, to be detestable, 1Ch 21:6 Job 15:16.

Derivative, תֹּועֵבָה.
